title: 9 Herbal Creams For Hypothyroidism
intro_desc: Herbal creams for hypothyroidism have gained popularity as a natural remedy to manage symptoms of the condition. These creams often contain a blend of herbs that work together to support thyroid function and improve overall health. One key herb used in these creams is Panax ginseng, which is known for its ability to boost energy and vitality. It helps to increase the production of thyroid hormones, which can become sluggish in individuals with hypothyroidism.  Another herb commonly used in these creams is Astragalus membranaceus. This herb has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to support the immune system and improve digestion. In the context of hypothyroidism, Astragalus has been shown to stimulate the production of thyroid hormones and improve the overall functioning of the thyroid gland.  Curcuma longa, or turmeric, is also a key ingredient in many herbal creams for hypothyroidism. Curcuma longa contains a compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and promote its proper functioning.  Using herbal creams for hypothyroidism can bring numerous benefits to your life. By supporting thyroid function and reducing symptoms of the condition, these creams can improve your energy levels, mental clarity, and overall well-being. Additionally, they can help to reduce symptoms of fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance that are commonly associated with hypothyroidism. By incorporating herbal creams into your daily routine, you may find that you're able to manage your symptoms more effectively and improve your quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams contains bioactive constituents like ginsenosides and eleutherosides that have been shown to have a positive effect on hypothyroidism. Ginsenosides have been found to stimulate the production of thyroid hormones by increasing the activity of enzymes involved in hormone synthesis. Eleutherosides, on the other hand, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often associated with hypothyroidism. The antioxidant properties of ginsenosides and eleutherosides can also help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative damage, promoting a healthy environment for thyroid function. By stimulating hormone production and reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Panax ginseng creams may help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal creams for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, and 2 teaspoons of Panax ginseng extract.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval until melted.
Add the shea butter and olive oil to the melted mixture and stir until combined.
Add the Panax ginseng extract to the mixture and stir well. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Pour the mixture into a small container or tin and let cool completely before use. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams contains bioactive constituents like astragalosides and polysaccharides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can be beneficial for people with hypothyroidism. Astragalosides also have a regulatory effect on the immune system, which can help reduce the autoimmune response that can cause the thyroid gland to underproduce hormones. The antioxidants in Astragalus membranaceus creams can also help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress, which can damage the gland and worsen hypothyroidism symptoms. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Astragalus membranaceus creams may help support thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal creams for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Astragalus membranaceus dried root, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add 1/2 cup of dried Astragalus membranaceus root to the melted oil and beeswax mixture. Stir well and let it infuse for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the infused oil mixture. Stir well and pour into a clean container. Let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is its active constituent. Curcumin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. This is beneficial for people with hypothyroidism, as chronic inflammation and oxidative stress can contribute to thyroid dysfunction. Curcuma longa creams also contain other active constituents like demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which have been shown to improve thyroid function by increasing the production of thyroid hormones. The anti-inflammatory properties of curcumin in Curcuma longa creams can also help reduce thyroid gland inflammation, which can improve thyroid function.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa), and 10 drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and add the turmeric powder and vitamin E oil. Stir well until the powder is fully dissolved.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, use a spoon to scoop out the Curcuma longa cream as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin, a bioactive constituent that helps reduce inflammation and promotes the production of thyroid hormones. Glycyrrhizin also has anti-oxidant properties, which protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals. Another key constituent, licoricidin, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the thyroid gland and promote the uptake of iodine, a essential nutrient for thyroid hormone production. The anti-stress properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams, particularly due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, help reduce cortisol levels, which can contribute to hypothyroidism. By reducing inflammation, promoting thyroid hormone production, and protecting the thyroid gland, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may be beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of licorice root extract (Glycyrrhiza glabra), and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
In a separate bowl, mix the distilled water and licorice root extract until well combined.
Slowly add the water mixture to the melted coconut oil mixture and stir well. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly.
Add the vitamin E oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely before using.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains bioactive constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce thyroid inflammation. These compounds also exhibit antioxidant activity, protecting the thyroid gland from oxidative stress. The volatile oils in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, increase blood flow and stimulate thyroid function, supporting the production of thyroid hormones. Zingiber officinale creams may also contain other active compounds like 6-gingerol, which has been shown to reduce thyroid antibodies and improve thyroid function in some studies. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and stimulating thyroid function, Zingiber officinale creams may provide relief from hypothyroidism symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale powder, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Add Zingiber officinale powder and vitamin E oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely before use as a topical cream for hypothyroidism.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera creams contains bioactive constituents like withanolides and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland, a common issue in hypothyroidism. Withanolides, in particular, have been shown to increase thyroid hormone production and improve thyroid function. The antioxidant properties of withaferin A and withanone help protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and improving thyroid function, Withania somnifera creams may provide relief from hypothyroidism symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Withania somnifera herbal creams for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Withania somnifera roots, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Grind the Withania somnifera roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground roots with coconut oil in a saucepan and heat over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add beeswax to the saucepan and continue heating, stirring until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove from heat and let cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a container, and store in the fridge.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains silymarin, a bioactive compound that helps with hypothyroidism. Silymarin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which protect the thyroid gland from damage and promote its proper functioning. It also contains flavonoids, such as silychristin and silydianin, that enhance the production of thyroid hormones. The sesquiterpenes in silymarin, like silibinin, improve the absorption of thyroid medications and reduce the risk of iodine deficiency. By supporting thyroid health, Silybum marianum creams can help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism, such as fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 100g of dried Silybum marianum flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil.
Mince the dried flowers and mix with the coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the mixture, then stir until combined and pour into a container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are active constituents that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce oxidative stress, which is often associated with hypothyroidism. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland. Additionally, the terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba, including bilobalide and ginkgolides, have been found to enhance thyroid function by stimulating the production of thyroid hormones. By improving thyroid function and reducing oxidative stress, Ginkgo biloba creams may help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in the double boiler over low heat.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove the double boiler from heat and stir in the Ginkgo biloba extract.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify before using as a cream for hypothyroidism.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis creams contains a rich blend of bioactive constituents, including schisandrins, lignans, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to support thyroid function. The schisandrins in Schisandra chinensis creams have antioxidant properties, which help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage. The lignans in this cream have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and promote a healthy thyroid environment. The polysaccharides in Schisandra chinensis creams have immunomodulatory properties, which can help regulate the immune system's response to thyroid-related inflammation. By addressing oxidative stress, inflammation, and immune system dysregulation, Schisandra chinensis creams may help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism and support thyroid health.
remedies.image_alt: Schisandra chinensis herbal creams for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 100g of dried Schisandra chinensis berries and 200g of coconut oil or other carrier oil.
Grind the dried Schisandra chinensis berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground Schisandra chinensis powder with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax and shea butter melt. Remove from heat.
Add 10 tablespoons of the melted mixture to 100g of coconut oil and mix well. Pour into a container and let cool.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent hypothyroidism, but its effectiveness is not fully understood. Some herbal teas, like ashwagandha and bladderwrack, contain iodine and other nutrients that support thyroid health. However, more research is needed to confirm their impact on preventing hypothyroidism. Iodine deficiency is a known cause of the condition.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to your hypothyroidism treatment, but it's essential to be cautious. Consuming them daily can interact with medications or worsen symptoms. Start with small amounts and monitor your body's response. Some herbs like ashwagandha and bladderwrack may help, but others like kava and goldenseal can be problematic.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to managing hypothyroidism. Some people notice improvements in energy levels and mood within a few weeks of regular consumption. As the body absorbs the nutrients, symptoms like weight gain and fatigue may start to subside. Results can vary, but noticeable changes often occur within 6-12 weeks.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for hypothyroidism is best in the morning. This is because it can help boost your metabolism and energy levels throughout the day. Many herbal teas contain ingredients like ashwagandha and ginseng that can stimulate your thyroid and get you ready for the day ahead.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas, like those containing goiter-reducing plants like bladderwrack or kelp, may affect hypothyroidism medication. These teas can slow down the body's ability to remove the medication, making it build up to toxic levels. This is because both the tea and the medication target the thyroid gland, potentially causing interactions.
faq_pregnant: While pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially those for hypothyroidism. Some teas, like kelp and bladderwrack, contain iodine, which is good for the thyroid but can be too much for the baby. Others, like ashwagandha, may affect the baby's development. It's best to stick to plain, pregnancy-safe herbal teas like peppermint or chamomile.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for hypothyroidism can interact with thyroid medications. If you're breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious. Some herbs like kelp and bladderwrack can affect thyroid function, which might impact your baby's thyroid development. It's also possible for certain herbs to pass into breast milk, but this is generally not a concern with moderate tea consumption.
title_2: 9 Natural Creams for Women to Manage Hypothyroidism
